description: Evolution tactics with evidence chains -- how the author self-manages intellectual transformation. Shows the 10+ tactic types detected by the evolution_tactics_detector engine with supporting quotes and patterns.

slots.label: Ideas Involved
slots.link_title_pattern: See idea {value} in Idea Evolution Map

group_descriptions.conceptual_recycling: Repurposing earlier concepts under new theoretical guises
group_descriptions.silent_revision: Revising positions without acknowledging the change
group_descriptions.selective_continuity: Maintaining some threads while quietly dropping others
group_descriptions.retroactive_framing: Reinterpreting past work through a later lens
group_descriptions.escalation: Progressively intensifying a claim or position over time
group_descriptions.narrative_bootstrapping: Building authority by narrating one's own trajectory
group_descriptions.framework_migration: Moving core ideas into a different theoretical home
group_descriptions.condition_shift: Redefining what counts as a precondition for one's argument
group_descriptions.biographical_teleology: Treating an intellectual career as if it always aimed at the present
group_descriptions.strategic_amnesia: Selectively forgetting earlier commitments that conflict with current ones
group_descriptions.vocabulary_migration: Shifting key terms to new semantic fields
group_descriptions.position_reversal: Adopting a stance previously argued against

planner_hint: Primary for thinkers known for strategy shifts, self-revision, or intellectual reinvention. Shows 10+ tactic types with evidence.
